---
title: 'catalog replay'
---

Replay the active catalog's operation log into a target catalog. Useful for
mirroring a catalog with a different remote backend, or for migrating between
storage providers.

## Usage

```bash
xorq catalog replay <target_path> [OPTIONS]
```

## Arguments

- **`target_path`**—Filesystem path where the target catalog will be initialized.

## Options

| Option | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--env-file` | none | Env file describing the target's annex remote. Mutually exclusive with `--env-prefix`. |
| `--env-prefix` | none | Env-var prefix describing the target's annex remote. Mutually exclusive with `--env-file`. |
| `--gcs` | off | Apply GCS defaults to the S3 remote config. |
| `--remote-url` | none | Git remote URL to set on the target catalog (pushes after replay). |
| `--preserve-commits` / `--no-preserve-commits` | `--preserve-commits` | Preserve original commit authors and timestamps. |
| `--force` | off | Force-push to the target's remote. |
| `--dry-run` | off | Print the replay plan without executing it. |

## Examples

Preview the operations that would be replayed:
```bash
xorq catalog replay ./mirrored-catalog --dry-run
```

Replay into a new catalog and push to a fresh remote:
```bash
xorq catalog replay ./mirrored-catalog \
    --env-file .env.target.s3 \
    --remote-url git@github.com:acme/mirror-catalog.git
```

## See also

- [`catalog log`](log.qmd)—view the operation history that will be replayed
